up.method: Sole carbon source (10 mM sodium acetate); specific uptake rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Lin et al. Interspecific competition prevents the proliferation of social cheaters in an unstructured environment. ISME J (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrad038. [PMC10939377]
growth_records.snippet: using the experimentally determined growth rate of cheater [beta_B = 0.75]... CNB-2/DeltaLuxR exhibited a significantly elevated growth rate (Wilcox-test, P < .05 across all initial cell densities)... Key parameter values were: beta_max=0.5
growth_records.notes: CNB-2/DeltaLuxR is the faster-growing 'cheater' strain. Reported max per capita growth rate on 10 mM acetate MSM: beta_B = 0.75 h-1 (called 'experimentally determined growth rate of cheater', used in Fig 3B). doubling_time = ln2/0.75 = 0.92 h. CAVEAT/inconsistency: the Materials-and-Methods parameter block lists beta_max = 0.5 h-1 for the cheater, contradicting beta_B=0.75 in Fig 3B and the biology (cheater faster than cooperator); value taken as 0.75 h-1 per the explicitly 'experimentally determined' statement. These are Lotka-Volterra model per-capita rates derived from the paper's experimental system, not directly-tabulated batch mu, hence low confidence. Absolute effective growth rates vs inoculum density are only in Fig 1A/2A. No qS/qP reported.

up.method: Sole carbon source (10 mM sodium acetate); specific uptake rate not reported

growth_records.cite: Lin et al. Interspecific competition prevents the proliferation of social cheaters in an unstructured environment. ISME J (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrad038. [PMC10939377]
growth_records.snippet: The KF-1 variant, although a weaker competitor with a slower inherent growth rate, grew better in SMX environments... When varying growth rate beta_B in Fig. 3B, we held alpha_A = 0.5 constant
growth_records.notes: KF-1 is the slower-growing 'cooperator' strain. Max per capita growth rate on 10 mM acetate MSM taken as alpha_A = 0.5 h-1 (value held constant for cooperator in Fig 3B). doubling_time = ln2/0.5 = 1.39 h. CAVEAT/inconsistency: the Methods parameter block lists alpha_max = 0.75 h-1 for the cooperator, which contradicts Fig 3B (alpha_A=0.5) and the stated biology that KF-1 grows slower than cheater CNB-2; adopted 0.5 h-1 to stay consistent with the cheater(0.75)>cooperator ordering. Lotka-Volterra model per-capita rate from the paper's experimental system, not a directly tabulated batch mu -> low confidence. No qS/qP reported.

growth_records.medium: BSM (basal salt medium) with phenol as sole carbon and energy source, chemostat feed 1500 mg/L phenol

up.method: steady-state mass balance qV=D*(S0-S); S0=1500 mg/L=15.94 mM, residual S=0.13 mM, D=0.1/day=0.004167/h

up.units: mM [g dry cell]-1 (as reported; missing time dimension in source)
up.method: O2-electrode phenol-oxygenating activity, intact cells, KCN-suppressed respiration; Michaelis-Menten/Haldane fit

yields.Yxs: not derivable in gDW/gS (no cell-dry-weight-per-cell given)

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.snippet: the chemostat culture was continuously supplied with BSM medium containing phenol (1,500 mg L-1) at a flow rate of 6.25 mL h-1, which corresponded to a dilution rate (D) of 0.1 day-1 ... temperature and pH were maintained at 25C and 7.0 ...
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat: mu=D=0.1/day=0.1/24=0.004167 h-1; doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.004167=166.3 h (=6.93 d). Phenol feed 1500 mg/L / MW(94.11)=15.94 mM; residual 0.13+/-0.010 mM stable to day 32; consumed=15.94-0.13=15.81 mM; volumetric phenol uptake qV=D*(S0-S)=0.004167*15.81=0.0659 mmol/L/h. Biomass-specific qS(mmol/gDW/h) NOT computed: only cell counts given (OD600 0.1=5.0e8 cells/mL, so ODmax 0.96=4.8e9 cells/mL) with no gDW-per-cell, so gDW/L unknown. Whole-cell kinetic Vmax (O2 electrode) reported per g dry cell but source omits the per-hour term; Ks(phenol)=0.70 uM control -> 1.3 uM with inhibitory H2O supernatant fraction; Ks(catechol)=14 uM -> 36 uM. Catechol not detected in bulk medium (fully channelled).

growth_records.medium: BSM2.0phe (BSM + 2.0 mM phenol) - batch positive control

up.method: phenol fully consumed (below detection after plateau) but time-resolved uptake rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.snippet: Growth parameters, including the lag time (h), growth rate constant (mu [h-1]), and amount of growth in the stationary phase (ODmax) ... Positive control 10+/-1.2 (lag) 0.25+/-0.0064 (mu) 0.40+/-0.0093 (OD600)
growth_records.notes: Table 2 positive control (phenol only). mu=0.25+/-0.0064 h-1 (reported, from growth curve). td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.25=2.77 h. lag=10 h; ODmax=0.40; specific growth activity U=0.048. Precultured/incubated at 25C in BSM2.0phe pH 7.2.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 negative control (phenol + collapsed-culture supernatant). mu=0.17+/-0.011 h-1 reported; td=0.693/0.17=4.08 h. Supernatant suppresses mu vs positive control (0.25->0.17), demonstrating feedback growth inhibition. ODmax 0.40; U=0.027.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 L-arginine. mu=0.29+/-0.0074 h-1; td=0.693/0.29=2.39 h. ODmax 0.32; U=0.044. L-arginine rescued growth from supernatant inhibition (negative growth effect only 3.2% vs 42% control).

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 Arg+SN. mu=0.25+/-0.013 h-1; td=0.693/0.25=2.77 h. mu essentially unchanged vs Arg alone -> L-arginine neutralizes supernatant inhibition.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 L-ornithine. mu=0.26+/-0.0065 h-1; td=0.693/0.26=2.67 h. Partial rescue (negative growth effect 34%).

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 Orn+SN. mu=0.19+/-0.031 h-1; td=0.693/0.19=3.65 h. Supernatant still inhibits (mu drops 0.26->0.19), i.e. ornithine does not fully rescue.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 L-citrulline. mu=0.26+/-0.0057 h-1; td=0.693/0.26=2.67 h. Negative growth effect 40% -> poor rescue.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 Ctr+SN. mu=0.22+/-0.0090 h-1; td=0.693/0.22=3.15 h. Supernatant lowers mu 0.26->0.22.

up.method: glutamate serves as additional C/N source; ODmax rose ~3-fold to 1.2 vs 0.40 control (glutamate consumed as substrate, two-step growth)

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.snippet: Glu 4.8+/-0.29 (lag) 0.35+/-0.0093 (mu) 1.2+/-0.036 (OD600) 0.41 (U) 59+/-6.3 (neg growth effect %). ODmax in the presence of L-glutamate and L-glutamine increased to ~3-fold that of control ... two-step growth was observed
growth_records.notes: Table 2 L-glutamate. mu=0.35+/-0.0093 h-1 (highest; second-phase of two-step growth); td=0.693/0.35=1.98 h. ODmax 1.2 (~3x control 0.40) because glutamate is consumed as an extra carbon/nitrogen substrate. Note negative growth effect still 59% -> glutamate did not neutralize inhibition despite boosting biomass.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 Glu+SN. mu=0.25+/-0.0047 h-1; td=0.693/0.25=2.77 h. mu drops 0.35->0.25 with supernatant; ODmax stays 1.2.

up.method: glutamine consumed as extra C/N source; ODmax ~1.2 vs 0.40 control

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 L-glutamine. mu=0.26+/-0.0040 h-1; td=0.693/0.26=2.67 h. ODmax 1.2 (~3x control) as glutamine is metabolized as substrate. Negative growth effect 27%.

growth_records.cite: Mohd Din, Abd Rahman Jabir et al. Imbalance in Carbon and Nitrogen Metabolism in Comamonas testosteroni R2 Is Caused by Negative Feedback and Rescued by L-arginine. Microbes and Environments (2021). DOI:10.1264/jsme2.ME21050. [PMC8674442]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 Gln+SN. mu=0.22+/-0.0051 h-1; td=0.693/0.22=3.15 h. ODmax 1.3; supernatant lowers mu 0.26->0.22.
